title: National pharmacare bilateral agreements - Canada.ca
content:
## National pharmacare bilateral agreements
The Government of Canada has signed funding agreements with four provinces and territories to make a range of contraception and diabetes medications free.
If you live in a province or territory with an agreement, those products will be available for free or at low cost at the pharmacy counter. You won't need to coordinate benefits with private insurance plans. Other fees, such as prescribing fees or delivery fees, may still apply.
Through these agreements, we've also provided funding that will help you access the supplies you need to:
- manage and monitor your diabetes
- administer your medication
- Who's covered by national pharmacare

## Agreements and coverage by province or territory
- Universal Access to Contraception and Diabetes Medications and Increased Access to Diabetes Devices and Supplies, Canada-British Columbia Funding Agreement News release: Government of Canada signs pharmacare agreement with British Columbia to improve universal access to free medications (March 6, 2025)
- News release: Government of Canada signs pharmacare agreement with British Columbia to improve universal access to free medications (March 6, 2025)
- Universal Access to Contraception and Diabetes Medications and Increased Access to Diabetes Devices and Supplies, Canada-Manitoba Funding Agreement News release: Government of Canada signs pharmacare agreement with Manitoba to improve affordable access to medications (February 27, 2025)
- News release: Government of Canada signs pharmacare agreement with Manitoba to improve affordable access to medications (February 27, 2025)
- Universal Access to Contraception and Diabetes Medications and Increased Access to Diabetes Devices and Supplies, Canada-Prince Edward Island Funding Agreement News release: Government of Canada signs pharmacare agreement with Prince Edward Island to improve universal access to free medications (March 7, 2025)
- News release: Government of Canada signs pharmacare agreement with Prince Edward Island to improve universal access to free medications (March 7, 2025)
- Universal Access to Contraception and Diabetes Medications and Increased Access to Diabetes Devices and Supplies, Canada-Yukon Funding Agreement News release: Government of Canada signs pharmacare agreement with Yukon to improve universal access to free medications (March 20, 2025)
- News release: Government of Canada signs pharmacare agreement with Yukon to improve universal access to free medications (March 20, 2025)
